Definition (EN): A port definition is a kind of occurrence definition (see 7.9 ) that defines a connection point to enable interactions between occurrences (most commonly parts).
Context/Example (EN): A port definition is a kind of occurrence definition (see 7.9 ) that defines a connection point to enable interactions between occurrences (most commonly parts).

Definition (EN): A port usage is a kind of occurrence usage definition that is a usage of a port definition.
Context/Example (EN): A port usage is a kind of occurrence usage definition that is a usage of a port definition.

Definition (EN): Each port definition has a conjugated port definition whose directed features are conjugate to those of the original port definition.
Context/Example (EN): Each port definition has a conjugated port definition whose directed features are conjugate to those of the original port definition.

Definition (EN): A conjugated port usage is a usage of a conjugated port definition.
Context/Example (EN): A conjugated port usage is a usage of a conjugated port definition.

Definition (EN): The conjugate of direction in is out and vice versa, while direction inout is its own conjugate.
Context/Example (EN): The conjugate of direction in is out and vice versa, while direction inout is its own conjugate.

Definition (EN): Two ports are said to conform if each feature of one port has a matching feature on the other port.
Context/Example (EN): In this case, if the two ports are connected, it is possible to have a flow between every directed feature of one port and the matching feature on the other port.

Definition (EN): The name of the conjugated port definition is always given by the name of the original port definition with the character ~ prepended, in the namespace of the original port definition.
Context/Example (EN): For example, if a port definition has the name P, then its conjugated port definition has the name P::'~P'.

Equivalent (JP): 共役ポート使用 (省略記法)
Definition (EN): A conugated port usage is a shorthand for declaring a port usage defined by a conjugated port definition. With this shorthand, rather than using the actual name of the conjugated port definition, the name of the original port definition can be used, preceded by the symbol ~.
Context/Example (EN): For example, port p : ~P; is equivalent to port p : P::'~P';
